title: Chicken Keema
tagline: Lean, high-protein keema from home-minced chicken breast, khade masale, and chicken masala.
macroNotes: Estimated from ingredients — 500 g chicken breast delivers ~115 g protein across the batch.
This is about as clean as a gym meal gets: ~59 g protein per serving, low fat, moderate carbs. Doesn't need much cutting.
Already peak protein, so soya is optional here — but you can stretch it further and cheaper by adding 50 g dry soya granules (soaked, squeezed dry) with the mince.

items: 500 g chicken breast, cut into chunks and pulsed to a coarse mince in a mixer grinder
1.5 tbsp chicken masala (MDH or your usual)
Optional dhaba touch: 1 tsp crushed kasuri methi / 1/2 tsp garam masala / 1 tsp butter

steps.title: Mince the chicken
steps.body: Cut 500 g chicken breast into cubes and pulse in a mixer grinder in short bursts — stop the moment it's a coarse mince, do not run it to a paste or the keema turns rubbery. Chilling the chicken first gives a cleaner mince.

steps.title: Temper the whole spices
steps.body: Heat 1 tbsp oil/ghee in a pan. Add 1 bay leaf, 1 inch cinnamon, 2 cloves, 2 green cardamom and 1 tsp cumin seeds; let them splutter 20 seconds.

steps.title: Brown the onions
steps.body: Add the 2 finely chopped onions with a pinch of salt and cook till properly golden brown — don't rush this, give it 7-8 minutes. This is the flavour base.

steps.title: Aromatics
steps.body: Add 1 tbsp ginger-garlic paste and 2 green chillies; cook 1 minute until the raw smell goes.

steps.title: Tomato masala
steps.body: Add the 2 chopped tomatoes and cook till soft and the oil separates. Add 1 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp red chilli, 1 tsp coriander powder, 1 tsp cumin powder and 1.5 tbsp chicken masala; cook 2 minutes.

steps.title: Add the mince
steps.body: Add the coarse chicken mince and break up the lumps aggressively with the spatula. Cook on medium-high until the colour changes fully. (If adding soya, stir in 50 g soaked, squeezed-dry soya granules now.)

steps.title: Moisture and simmer
steps.body: Stir in 2 tbsp curd + 1/4 cup water and simmer 8-10 minutes. Breast is lean — pull it off the heat as soon as it's cooked through, over-simmering dries it out. Taste for salt.

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Stir in a handful of chopped coriander and a squeeze of lemon. For dhaba flavour, crush in 1 tsp kasuri methi or 1/2 tsp garam masala, or swirl in 1 tsp butter.

notes: Breast mince turns rubbery if over-processed or overcooked — pulse coarse and stop cooking the moment it's done.
Dry gym-meal version: skip excess tomato water and cook till crumbly and dry; eat with rice or roti.
Juicy wrap version: keep it a little saucy and stuff into a roti with sliced onions and a spoon of curd.
source: Adapted from my own cooking notes.
